title: Hippeastrum ischihualastum
intro: Hippeastrum ischihualastum, also known as the Night-Blooming Hippeastrum, is a species of flowering bulbous plant native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its showy, trumpet-shaped flowers that bloom at night. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hippeastrum ischihualastum include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as being used as a topical treatment for skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Hippeastrum ischihualastum is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, commonly known as the amaryllis family. It is native to the tropical regions of Mexico and Central America. The classification of Hippeastrum ischihualastum places it within the genus Hippeastrum, which comprises about 90 species of flowering plants.
